100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On May 20, 1926, the British General Strike entered its second week, with workers across various sectors continuing to demand better wages and conditions, disrupting daily life and raising concerns over national stability. In Germany, political tensions escalated as the government faced criticism for its handling of economic recovery post-World War I. Meanwhile, in the United States, the film industry celebrated the success of "The Ten Commandments," showcasing the growing influence of cinema in American culture.
